zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle於1307年出生在英國蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾度巴頓時，他的父親Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun41歲，母親Gilchrist Mure38歲。他於大約1330年與Mary Fleming在英國蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1350年在蘇格蘭登巴頓史爾丹尼斯托享年43歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle于1307年出生在英国苏格兰登巴頓史爾度巴頓时，他的父亲Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun41岁，母亲Gilchrist Mure38岁。他于大约1330年与Mary Fleming在英国苏格兰结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1350年在苏格兰登巴頓史爾丹尼斯托享年43岁。

nameMeanings.name: Governor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: American English: occupational name for a member of the householdof a governor.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

nameMeanings.name: Dumbar
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from Dunbar, a place on the North Sea coast near Edinburgh, named with Gaelic dùn ‘fort’ + barr ‘top, summit’.

History: A family of this name were granted the lands of Dunbar by Malcolm III (1058–1093), and later acquired the earldoms of Dunbar and March. These earldoms were then withdrawn from the family by James I in 1434.

nameMeanings.name: Castle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name from Anglo-Norman French, Middle English castel ‘castle, fortified building or set of buildings’, especially the residence of a feudal lord (from Late Latin castellum, a diminutive of castrum ‘fort, Roman walled city’). The name would also have denoted a servant who lived and worked at such a place, or someone who had an obligation to provide services or rent to a castle.

variants.name: Castell
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Roberto, Amarilys, Carlos, Eduardo, Estevan, Francisco, Guadalupe, Humberto, Jorge, Manuel, Mateo, Pablo.
English and Scottish: variant of   Castle  .

Manx: shortened form of Gaelic Mac Asgaill ‘son of Ásketill’, an Old Norse personal name composed of the elements áss ‘god’ + ketill ‘sacrificial cauldron, helmet’. The Manx surname is cognate with Scottish Gaelic McAskill .

variants.name: Cassell
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name of Norman origin, from Cassel in the Nord department, Flanders, France.
English: sometimes a variant of   Castle  .
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Ó Caisile, which is probably in origin a variant of Ó Caiside, see Cassidy .

variants.name: Castel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Benoit, Herve, Jean Michel, Thierry, Yves.  Spanish Jose, Blanco, Carlos, Rafael.
Aragonese, Catalan, and Jewish (Sephardic): variant of   Castell   3, a cognate of 5 below.

variants.name: Castles
meanings: English: variant of Castle , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish (Armagh and Meath): in Ireland, this may be the English name, but it may also be an Anglicized form of Ó Caisil; see Cashell .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle was born in 1307, in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun, was 41 and his mother, Gilchrist Mure, was 38. He married Mary Fleming about 1330, in Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1350, in Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, at the age of 43.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle est né en 1307, à Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Gilchrist Mure, avait 38 ans. Il a épousé Mary Fleming environ 1330, à Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1350, à Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 43 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle 1307 in Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun 41 und seine Mutter Gilchrist Mure 38. Er hat um 1330 in Schottland, United Kingdom Mary Fleming geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1350 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle è nato il nel 1307 nel Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Gilchrist Mure, aveva 38 anni. Lui sposato Mary Fleming circa 1330, nel Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1350, nel Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, all'età di 43 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castleが1307年にイギリススコットランドダンバートンシュアイアダンバートンで生まれた時，父Sir Hugh de Danzielstounは41歳，母Gilchrist Mureは38歳であった。約1330年にイギリススコットランドでMary Flemingと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1350年にスコットランドダンバートンシュアイアデニーストウンで43歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle(이)가 1307년 영국 스코틀랜드 던바토실르 드바턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Gilchrist Mure(은)는 38세였다. 그는 대략 1330년 영국 스코틀랜드에서 Mary Fleming(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1350년 스컷랜드 던바토실르 데니스타운에서 43세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle nasceu em 1307, em Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Gilchrist Mure, tinha 38 anos. Ele casou-se com Mary Fleming aproximadamente 1330, em Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1350, em Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, com 43 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle родился в 1307 г. в местности думбартон, дунбартоншир, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Gilchrist Mure, 38. Он женился на женщине по имени Mary Fleming около 1330 г. в местности Шотландия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1350 г. в местности деннистоун, дунбартоншир, Шотландия в возрасте 43 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John Danzielstoun - Governor of Dumbar Castle nació en 1307, en Dumbarton, Dunbartonshire, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir Hugh de Danzielstoun, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Gilchrist Mure, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Mary Fleming aproximadamente 1330, en Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1350, en Dennystown, Dunbartonshire, Scotland, a la edad de 43 años.
